Computation: 2024-2025\">Quantum Processes and Computation: 2024-2025</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->The textbook for this course is <strong>Picturing Quantum Processes: A First Course in Quantum Theory and Diagrammatic Reasoning</strong>. It should be available in many libraries throughout the university and most online bookstores. The ebook PDF is also available for free to all members of Oxford University&nbsp;...<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[6--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> Phillip Kaye Raymond Laﬂamme Michele Mosca\">An Introduction to Quantum Computing Phillip Kaye Raymond Laﬂamme Michele Mosca</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->this section we familiarize the reader with the algebraic notation used in quantum · mechanics, remind the reader of some basic facts about complex vector spaces,<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">University of Oxford</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">cs.ox.ac.uk <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› teaching  › courses  › 2025-2026  › quantum</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Processes and Computation: 2025-2026\">Quantum Processes and Computation: 2025-2026</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->This includes a structural understanding of some basic quantum mechanics, understanding of the high-level structure of most well-known quantum algorithms in the circuit model as well as important protocols such as quantum teleportation, and some basic understanding of state-of-the-art techniques for quantum compilation and classical simulation of quantum computing.<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[6--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> Academic</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">academic.oup.com <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› book  › 54860</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Mechanics: A New Introduction | Oxford Academic\">Quantum Mechanics: A New Introduction | Oxford Academic</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">March 5, 2009 -</span><!--]--> <!---->2026. ... This book <strong>provides the reader with a contemporary and comprehensive introduction to Quantum Mechanics</strong>. It is suitable for beginners as well as for more advanced university students.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a of Oxford</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">cs.ox.ac.uk <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› teaching  › courses  › 2019-2020  › cqm</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Categorical Quantum Mechanics: 2019-2020\">Categorical Quantum Mechanics: 2019-2020</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->The course notes have been recently published by OUP as the textbook Categories for <strong>Quantum Theory: An Introduction</strong>. Students may wish to obtain this book to accompany the course. However, full notes are also available for download. This form is not to be used by students studying for a degree&nbsp;...<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[6--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> of Oxford</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">cs.ox.ac.uk <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› teaching  › courses  › 2020-2021  › quantum</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Processes and Computation: 2020-2021\">Quantum Processes and Computation: 2020-2021</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->The textbook for this course is <strong>Picturing Quantum Processes: A First Course in Quantum Theory and Diagrammatic Reasoning</strong>. It should be available in many libraries throughout the university and most online bookstores. The ebook PDF is also available for free to all members of Oxford University&nbsp;...<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[6--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> of Oxford</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">cs.ox.ac.uk <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› teaching  › courses  › 2020-2021  › cqm</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Categorical Quantum Mechanics: 2020-2021\">Categorical Quantum Mechanics: 2020-2021</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->The course notes have been recently published by OUP as the textbook Categories for <strong>Quantum Theory: An Introduction</strong>. Students may wish to obtain this book to accompany the course. However, full notes are also available for download. This form is not to be used by students studying for a degree&nbsp;...<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[6--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> of Oxford</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">cs.ox.ac.uk <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› teaching  › courses  › 2019-2020  › quantum</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Computer Science\">Quantum Computer Science</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->This course provides an interdisciplinary introduction to the emerging field of quantum computer science, explaining basic quantum mechanics (including finite dimensional Hilbert spaces and their tensor products), quantum entanglement, its structure and its physical consequences (e.g.<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> of Oxford Podcasts</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">podcasts.ox.ac.uk <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› series  › quantum-mechanics</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Quantum Mechanics | University of Oxford Podcasts\">Quantum Mechanics | University of Oxford Podcasts</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!---->In this series of physics lectures, Professor J.J. Binney explains how probabilities are obtained from quantum amplitudes, why they give rise to quantum interference, the concept of a complete set of amplitudes and how this defines a &quot;quantum state&quot;. A book of the course can be obtained from http://bit.ly/binneybook<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Oxford Academic</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">academic.oup.com <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">› edited-volume  › 43513</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"The Oxford Handbook of the History of Quantum Interpretations | Oxford Academic\">The Oxford Handbook of the History of Quantum Interpretations | Oxford Academic</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">March 7, 2022 -</span><!--]--> <!---->This Handbook may serve those interested in the history of quantum mechanics and those who are engaged with, or attracted by current research on quantum information, as it may give them a flavor of the history that led to or lay the groundwork for the current investigations. Keywords: Quantum controversy, entanglement, decoherence, measurement problem, quantum information, quantum gravity, quantum field theories, Albert Einstein, Niels Bohr, John Bell ... Access to content on Oxford Academic is often provided through institutional subscriptions and purchases.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a class=\"result-content svelte-1rq4ngz\"><a href=\"https://global.oup.com/ushe/disciplines/chemistry/physical-chemistry-courses/quantum-mechanics-quantum-chemistry/\" target=\"_self\" class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" Physics</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span I. Lvovsky, Quantum Physics: An Introduction Based on Photons</strong> (Springer Amazon).<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a courses)<!----></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----> svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Oxford: building a quantum future, now | University of Oxford Department of Physics\">Oxford: building a quantum future, now | University of Oxford Department of Physics</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">January 5, 2026 -</span><!--]--> <!---->Researchers in the Department of Physics work across a range of quantum technologies. When it comes to quantum information and computation, we study how quantum-mechanical systems store and manipulate information.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://www.physics.ox.ac.uk/oxford-building-quantum-future-now\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--><!--]--><!--[5--><!--[0--><div class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f\" data-pos=\"20\" data-type=\"web\" data-keynav=\"true\"><div class=\"result-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><div class=\"result-content svelte-1rq4ngz\"><a href=\"https://physicsworld.com/a/teaching-quantum-physics-to-everyone-pictures-offer-a-new-way-of-understanding/\" target=\"_self\" class=\"svelte-14r20fy l1\"><div class=\"site-name-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"favicon-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><!--[0--><div class=\"favicon-background-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><img class=\"favicon-background svelte-on1hvy\" src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/hMYt2_d-Xc3z0P4edpeJ9n54aR7gYhoGywQ7DJOCysA/rs:fit:32:32:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cDovL2Zhdmlj/b25zLnNlYXJjaC5i/cmF2ZS5jb20vaWNv/bnMvMDVkZTk0ZTE3/OGRmYzRmZTNjOGRh/NTQyN2ZhMTQzMGM2/NTkwNGE2NzgyYjE2/YTE1M2EzMzViZWVi/Y2U3MjdhOC9waHlz/aWNzd29ybGQuY29t/Lw\" loading=\"lazy\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div> <div class=\"site-name-content svelte-on1hvy\"><div class=\"desktop-small-semibold t-secondary text-ellipsis\">Physics World</div> <div class=\"url-wrapper svelte-on1hvy\"><cite class=\"snippet-url desktop-small-regular t-tertiary svelte-on1hvy\">physicsworld.com <span class=\"text-ellipsis\">  › home  › teaching quantum physics to everyone: pictures offer a new way of understanding</span></cite> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div></div> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!----> <div class=\"title search-snippet-title line-clamp-1 svelte-14r20fy\" title=\"Teaching quantum physics to everyone: pictures offer a new way of understanding – Physics World\">Teaching quantum physics to everyone: pictures offer a new way of understanding – Physics World</div></a><!----> <!--[0--><div class=\"generic-snippet svelte-1cwdgg3\"><div class=\"content desktop-default-regular t-primary line-clamp-dynamic svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><span class=\"t-secondary\">May 29, 2025 -</span><!--]--> <!---->In 2023, Oxford and Quantinuum joined forces to use the method in a pilot summer programme for 15 to 17 year-olds. Dhawan was one of their students. The books mentioned in the podcast are Picturing Quantum Processes: A First Course in Quantum Theory and Diagrammatic Reasoning by Bob Coecke and Aleks Kissinger; and Quantum in Pictures: A New Way to Understand the Quantum World by Bob Coecke and Stefano Gogioso.<!----></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1cwdgg3\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://physicsworld.com/a/teaching-quantum-physics-to-everyone-pictures-offer-a-new-way-of-understanding/\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/l5PXcz02Z3KClAFeYxY4jkNQHRJDYZ3Oz_s4JN48yhs/rs:fit:200:200:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cHM6Ly9waHlz/aWNzd29ybGQuY29t/L3dwLWNvbnRlbnQv/dXBsb2Fkcy8yMDI1/LzA1LzI5LTUtMjVx/dWFudHVtcGljdHVy/ZXNsaXN0LmpwZw\" alt=\"\" width=\"112\" height=\"112\" loading=\"lazy\" class=\"svelte-1yrspal\" onload=\"this.__e=event\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--><!----></a><!----><!--]--><!----> <!--[-1--><!--]--></div><!--]--></div><!--]--> <!--[0--><!--[-1--><!--]--><!--]--></div> <!--[0--><div class=\"thumbnail-wrapper svelte-1rq4ngz\"><!--[0--><!----><a href=\"https://physicsworld.com/a/teaching-quantum-physics-to-everyone-pictures-offer-a-new-way-of-understanding/\" class=\"thumbnail svelte-1yrspal general\" target=\"_self\" tabindex=\"-1\"><!--[0--><img src=\"https://imgs.search.brave.com/l5PXcz02Z3KClAFeYxY4jkNQHRJDYZ3Oz_s4JN48yhs/rs:fit:200:200:1:0/g:ce/aHR0cHM6Ly9waHlz/aWNzd29ybGQuY29t/L3dwLWNvbnRlbnQv/dXBsb2Fkcy8yMDI1/LzA1LzI5LTUtMjVx/dWFudHVtcGljdHVy/ZXNsaXN0LmpwZw\" alt=\"\" width=\"112\" height=\"112\" loading=\"lazy\" class=\"svelte-1yrspal\" onload=\"this.__e=event\" onerror=\"this.__e=event\"/><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[0--><div id=\"pagination-snippet\" class=\"snippet  svelte-jmfu5f standalone\"><div id=\"pagination\" class=\"svelte-14nrfpk\"><!--[-1--><!--]--> <!--[-1--><!--]-->
